title: EIP-8015: Remove deposit and eth1data fields
description: Removes the legacy deposits and eth1_data fields from BeaconBlockBody after EIP-6110 has been fully finalized. These fields become obsolete once all validators have transitioned to the new in-protocol deposit processing mechanism introduced by EIP-6110, eliminating deprecated validation logic and reducing technical debt from the transition period.
author: Terence (@terencechain)
laymanDescription: Cleans up the consensus layer by removing two data fields that were only needed during the transition to a newer deposit system. Since all deposits now go through the updated mechanism, these leftover fields serve no purpose and removing them simplifies the protocol.

endUsers.description: No user-facing impact. Deposit processing already uses the new mechanism; this change is purely internal cleanup.

appDevs.description: No impact on application developers. The removed fields were internal to consensus layer block processing.

walletDevs.description: No impact. Wallet interactions with staking deposits are unchanged; only internal beacon block structure is modified.

toolingInfra.description: Tools parsing BeaconBlockBody must update to the new structure without deposits and eth1_data fields.

layer2s.description: No direct impact. Layer 2 protocols do not interact with consensus layer deposit fields.

stakersNodes.description: No operational change for stakers. Deposit processing continues via EIP-6110; node operators need updated client software.

clClients.description: Must remove deposit and eth1_data processing from BeaconBlockBody, drop related validation logic, and update SSZ containers. Moderate refactoring effort.

elClients.description: Minimal impact. No consensus logic changes required; mainly fork-activation configuration and compatibility testing with updated CL clients.

benefits: Eliminates deprecated validation logic and code paths from block processing.
Reduces technical debt accumulated during the EIP-6110 transition period.
Simplifies the BeaconBlockBody structure for improved maintainability.
